title: Combinations
learning_outcomes: Derive mathematical and programmatic bounds for Selection formula properties ($^nC_r$), Grid path problems, partitions/distributions math (stars and bars).

content_sections.title: Combinations
content_sections.content:
### Arithmetic, Totients & Combinatorics
Quantitative Aptitude focuses on number relationships, algebraic systems, and probability calculations.

#### Euler's Totient Theorem
For coprimes $a$ and $n$, the remainder of $a^{\phi(n)} \pmod n$ is $1$, where $\phi(n)$ is Euler's totient (count of numbers coprime to $n$ up to $n$):
$$\phi(n) = n \prod_{p \mid n} \left(1 - \frac{1}{p}\right)$$
For primes, $\phi(p) = p - 1$, yielding Fermat's Little Theorem: $a^{p-1} \equiv 1 \pmod p$.

#### Product Consistency Constraint
If $A \times B = C$ and $C$ remains constant, a fractional increase in $A$ by $x/y$ requires a fractional decrease in $B$ by $\frac{x}{x + y}$ to maintain product consistency.

#### Stars-and-Bars Partitions
To distribute $n$ identical objects among $k$ distinct bins:
*   *Empty bins allowed:* $\binom{n + k - 1}{k - 1}$ partitions.
*   *No empty bins allowed:* $\binom{n - 1}{k - 1}$ partitions.

### Depth Analysis: Combinations
In production configurations, we analyze how Selection formula properties ($^nC_r$), Grid path problems, partitions/distributions math (stars and bars) affects performance parameters. We evaluate memory layouts and consensus thresholds.

callouts.title: Common Trap in Combinations
callouts.content: A common mistake when analyzing Combinations is ignoring latency bounds and boundary edge cases during concurrent executions.

callouts.title: Production Scaling for Combinations
callouts.content: Production servers implement strict resource isolation and cache alignments to prevent performance regressions when running Combinations loops.
